seoTitle: How to Track Mileage in QuickBooks Online Easily
metaDescription: Set up mileage tracking in QuickBooks Online to record trips accurately and maximize your business tax deductions with less manual effort.
intro: Tracking business mileage properly can mean real savings at tax time, but only if the records are accurate and complete. This guide walks through setting up mileage tracking in QuickBooks Online, recording trips with the right details, and understanding why this matters for deductions. It's particularly useful for freelancers, consultants, and small business owners who use their vehicle for work.

steps.heading: Set up mileage tracking
steps.body: Turn on and configure the mileage tracking feature within QuickBooks Online so you're ready to start logging trips.

steps.heading: Record trips in QuickBooks Online
steps.body: Add trip details as they happen, including the purpose and distance, to keep your mileage log accurate.

steps.heading: Understand the tax deduction benefits
steps.body: Accurate mileage tracking supports your deduction claims, helping ensure you're not leaving money on the table at tax time.

steps.heading: Integrate mileage tracking with other tools
steps.body: Connect mileage data with your broader financial tools in QuickBooks Online to reduce manual entry and keep records consistent.

faq.question: Do I need a separate app to track mileage with QuickBooks Online?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online includes mileage tracking as a built-in feature, so a separate app isn't required, though integrations can add convenience.

faq.question: What counts as deductible mileage?
faq.answer: Generally, business-related trips such as client visits or job-related travel qualify, but consult a tax professional for specifics on your situation.

faq.question: Can I track mileage from my phone?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online's mileage tracking is designed to work on the go, making it easier to log trips as they happen rather than reconstructing them later.

faq.question: How does accurate mileage tracking affect my tax return?
faq.answer: Well-documented mileage supports your deduction claims and reduces the risk of issues if your records are ever reviewed.
